text:
The Master of the Swamp (, Numa no Nushi) is a whale-sized fish living in the swamp of Whale Island. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 1, Chapter 1 

Appearance

It is a giant fish several times larger than Gon. It has the overall body of a scaly fish with sharp teeth, and oddly enough, it has the thin skeletal legs of an arthropod, with 16 legs on the left and right side of its belly (4 on each side in the 2011 anime adaptation).

In the manga, it is primarily red and pink in color, while in the 1999 anime adaptation, it has a dark red body with a white underbelly and yellowfins. In the 2011 anime adaptation, it is a blue fish with a light green face and underbelly, and dark blue fins. 

Plot

Hunter Exam arc

Despite its difficulty of capture, five fishermen were unable to reel him in at once, and Gon caught the fish at the age of eleven. 

Manga Appearances

Trivia

In Crunchyroll's translation of the 2011 anime adaptation, it is known as the "Lord of the Lake".

Anime and Manga Differences

In the Nippon Animation adaptation, Gon releases the fish after Mito signs his Hunter Exam application card. Hunter × Hunter - Episode 1 (1999) However, its fate is unknown in the manga and 2011 anime.
In the 2011 anime adaptation, Ging is said to have achieved the same feat when he was twelve. Hunter × Hunter - Episode 1 (2011) 
In Episode 4 of the Me & Roboco Anime, Roboco is seen wrestling the Master of the Swamp out of a stream.
